item.title: Ex-Credit Suisse Bankers Aim to Finance Venezuela Oil Revival
item.description: A private-credit firm founded by former Credit Suisse bankers is seeking to finance Venezuela’s oil services sector, as one of the country’s largest energy conferences in decades draws investors back to Caracas.

item.title: Grupo Mexico to Merge Power Unit With BlackRock-Backed Saavi
item.description: Mexican mining and railroad conglomerate Grupo Mexico has struck a deal to merge its electricity generation operations with a BlackRock Inc. unit, creating one of the country’s biggest private power operators.

item.title: Private Credit Boom Turns Fund Finance Into $1 Trillion Market
item.description: A boom in private credit drove the fund finance market past $1 trillion last year, as investment vehicles borrowed to manage liquidity and to bridge delayed exits, according to Moody’s Ratings.

item.title: Jeweler Claire’s to Shut All UK Stores After Latest Insolvency
item.description: Claire’s Accessories has stopped trading in the UK and Ireland, in the latest blow to the British retail industry.

item.title: Firms Swarm US Market With New Debt, Seizing Issuance Window
item.description: Firms swarmed the US primary markets with new debt on Monday, seizing a window to lock in borrowing costs ahead of a raft of earnings and central bank decisions, even as uncertainty in the Middle East lingers.

item.title: Wizz Air Has Enough Jet Fuel to Handle Strong Summer Bookings
item.description: Wizz Air Holdings Plc expects to have enough jet fuel for the next month — despite industry warnings about potential shortages — as the budget airline logs stronger summer bookings than last year.

item.title: ARC Resources Leads Canadian Energy Peers Higher on Shell Deal
item.description: ARC Resources Ltd. shares were on track for their biggest gain since 2020 on Monday, leading peers higher, after Shell Plc agreed to acquire the Canadian energy producer in a C$22 billion ($16 billion) deal.

item.title: Google’s AI Power Over Android Ecosystem Targeted by EU
item.description: Google was targeted by European Union watchdogs who unveiled a slate of proposals aimed at prising open its Android ecosystem to rivals’ AI services.

item.title: Hasbro ‘Magic: The Gathering’ Video Game Staff Try to Unionize
item.description: More than 100 employees at Hasbro Inc. who are involved in designing the video game rendition of the popular card game Magic: The Gathering are forming plans to unionize with the Communications Workers of America.

item.title: Stonepeak and Bernhard to Buy Cleco From Macquarie-Led Group
item.description: Stonepeak and Bernhard Capital Partners agreed to buy Cleco Group LLC from a consortium comprised of Macquarie Asset Management, British Columbia Investment Management Corporation and Manulife Investment Management.

item.title: Google Staff Urge Pichai to Refuse Classified Military AI Work
item.description: Hundreds of AI researchers at Alphabet Inc.’s Google have signed onto a letter urging Chief Executive Officer Sundar Pichai to refuse to make the company’s artificial intelligence systems available for classified workloads for US defense missions, according to organizers of the effort.

item.title: Italy’s Angelini Said to Explore Catalyst Pharma Acquisition
item.description: Italian drugmaker Angelini Pharma is exploring a potential acquisition of Catalyst Pharmaceuticals Inc. as it looks at deals to expand its footprint globally, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Apple Store Union Workers at Closing Location Accuse Company of Retaliation
item.description: Union representatives said Apple denied workers the ability to easily transfer to other locations, in a departure from its typical practice.

item.title: Chemical Firm Arxada Gets Key Lender Support for Debt Extension
item.description: Specialty chemical firm Arxada AG won the support of key creditors in its bid to delay debt repayments, as the broader industry faces stress from rising energy prices and subdued demand.

item.title: Vitol US Gasoline Traders Greer, Reiser Depart From Energy Giant
item.description: Two US gasoline traders have departed from Vitol Group, the world’s largest independent commodity trading house.

item.title: Mideast Urea Output Slumps With Lack of Fertilizer Ships to Load
item.description: More than half of the Middle East’s urea output may have been lost since the start of the Iran conflict, which is continuing to disrupt fertilizer flows from the region and threatening global food inflation.

item.title: Polish TikTok Star Beats MrBeast in Record Cancer Care Campaign
item.description: A Polish TikTok personality set a world record for a livestream charity campaign by raising at least 251 million zloty ($69 million) for a foundation helping children with cancer.

item.title: Deutsche, JPMorgan Favor Energy-Linked Currencies Amid Iran War
item.description: Investors are doubling down on bets that energy-linked currencies will keep rising as the US-Iran conflict reshapes global oil markets, with Deutsche Bank, JPMorgan Chase & Co. and Pioneer Investments urging clients to buy into the trade.

item.title: RBC Hires Graham, Boccio as Heads of CDS and Electronic Trading
item.description: Royal Bank of Canada is bolstering its credit trading business with senior hires, including new roles focused on credit-default swaps and electronic trading.

item.title: Citadel Securities Sees Markets Buoyed as Iran Risks Recede
item.description: Stocks and bonds can rally in tandem as the Iran conflict moves closer to a resolution that avoids escalation, even as the stalemate around the Strait of Hormuz continues, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: India Changes Bank Loan-Loss Rules to Align With Global Norms
item.description: India’s central bank has introduced tighter rules requiring banks to set aside more funds for future losses on credit and loan portfolios, in a move to align the sector’s norms with global standards.

item.title: Mexico Credit Rating Verdict From Moody’s Expected by June End
item.description: Mexico’s investment-grade credit rating faces a key decision by the end of June, when Moody’s Ratings is expected to resolve the negative outlook it assigned the nation in late 2024.

item.title: American Airlines to Sell $1.14 Billion in Bonds for 32 Planes
item.description: American Airlines Group Inc. on Monday is selling $1.14 billion of bonds financing a group of planes.

item.title: Meme-Stock Casualty Plotkin Joins Rich Shifting Assets Into ETFs
item.description: Hedge fund manager turned NBA owner Gabe Plotkin, who shut his firm after a bruising showdown with meme-stock traders, is planning to convert some of his own assets into an ETF using a tactic that’s helped a slew of wealthy investors defer tax.

item.title: Apple, Google Crushed California Bill That Helped Smaller Rivals
item.description: Some of the world’s largest technology companies swiftly quashed a California legislative measure that pitted giants like Apple Inc. and Google against smaller rivals, a display of their immense political influence in their home state.

item.title: NBA Push Into Vegas, Seattle Gains Steam With Potential Bidders
item.description: The National Basketball Association has received signs of interest from multiple investor groups for new teams in Las Vegas and Seattle that the league plans to sell for as much as $10 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Truist Loses Trio of Veteran Traders From Municipal Bond Team
item.description: Three members of Truist Financial Corp’s municipal bond department have departed the Charlotte-based bank.

item.title: Sequoia and Nvidia Back Ex-DeepMind Researcher’s New AI Startup at $5.1 Billion Value
item.description: Former Google DeepMind researcher David Silver has raised $1.1 billion in funding for his new artificial intelligence startup Ineffable Intelligence at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

item.title: Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square IPO Expected to Raise $5 Billion
item.description: Billionaire Bill Ackman’s IPO of his closed-end fund and his alternative asset management company is expected to raise about $5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, the low end of the targeted fundraising range.

item.title: Data Center Venture Bankrolled by Carlyle Co-Founder Looks to Norway’s Fjords
item.description: Carlyle Group Inc. co-founder Bill Conway is backing a new venture that aims to procure land and power in Norway and sell the assets to data-center developers and technology companies.
